ogTitle: Clifton Beach vs Clintonvale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Clifton Beach and Clintonvale.
heading: Clifton Beach vs Clintonvale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Clifton Beach, Queensland and Clintonvale, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Clifton Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Clintonvale (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Clifton Beach or Clintonvale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Clifton Beach has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Clintonvale scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Clifton Beach scores 41/100 for bushfire risk. Clintonvale scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Clifton Beach or Clintonvale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Clifton Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
